§6Nuclear Byproducts: Trinitite Glass§f

§aHarvest trinitite glass shards from the radioactive blast crater of a nuclear explosion!§f

Nuclear detonations are not just destructive; they trigger extreme thermodynamic pressures that fuse natural minerals into unique physical states. By deliberately preparing your blast site, you can harvest §9Trinitite Shards§f — an essential material for elite, late-game chemical and electrical engineering.

§6Harvesting Trinitite§f:
Trinitite glass is formed exclusively when sand is exposed to a nuclear detonation:
§b• The Critical Distance§f: You must place §bSand blocks§f at a distance of §ebetween 10 and 50 blocks§f from the detonation center of the bomb.
  - §cToo Close§f (≤ 10 blocks): Sand is §cvaporized entirely§f into empty air.
  - §cToo Far§f (≥ 50 blocks): Sand is unaffected and remains plain sand.
§b• Shard Harvesting§f: After triggering the explosion and waiting for the dust to settle, venture into the crater. Mine the translucent green §bTrinitite§f glass blocks to gather §bTrinitite Shards§f.
§b• Radioactive Earth§f: The blast site will be heavily contaminated with §cRadioactive Earth§f and toxic radiation levels. Ensure you wear a full §bHazmat Suit§f or a high-tier respirator before entering the crater!

§eTip§f: Witherite, processed from trinitite, is a critical late-game material. Craft Witherite to begin working with nuclear byproducts.
